Phase-64.NAME: Premium Resend Email Templates
Phase-64.objective: Redesign verification and password-reset Resend email templates in the premium KrotPN Matrix style.
Phase-64.non-goals: Changing Resend installer/deploy wiring, email provider semantics, verification token storage, or password policy.

steps.step-1: Audit current verification/reset HTML and text fallback templates.
steps.step-2: Define premium email layout with logo, dark-compatible styling, action button, safety copy, and compact content.
steps.step-3: Keep text fallback clean, readable, and independent of remote images.
steps.step-4: Preserve token redaction, one-time reset semantics, and provider error safety.
steps.step-5: Update backend email tests and Phase-64 template smoke requirements.
steps.step-6: Verify public logo URL and remote-image-blocking behavior assumptions.
steps.step-7: Run backend email tests, Phase-64 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected surface guard.
steps.step-8: Record evidence in M-079/V-M-079 and update M-040/M-062/M-069 if execution changes their contracts.

Phase-64.verification-design: Phase-64 is ready for execution only after V-M-079 gates prove email-client-safe HTML, readable text fallback, token redaction, public logo URL use, Resend/provider boundary safety, and protected deploy/install/runtime isolation.
Phase-64.acceptance: Email templates match the premium visual system while remaining transactional, token-safe, and readable.
Phase-64.evidence: 2026-06-04: Phase-64 upgraded verification and password-reset email templates to a compact premium Matrix-safe shell with public /brand/email-logo.png body logo, email-client-safe table/inline styling, clear CTA, image-blocking-safe fallback panel, plain text fallback without logo noise, Phase-64 log markers, backend email tests, Phase-64 smoke, Phase-51 brand regression, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/provider/runtime/frontend guard. Resend provider semantics, installer/deploy wiring, DNS/BIMI, auth tokens, billing, VPN, and MTProto runtime were untouched.
